LAW AND MOTION 302, "IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS CURRENTLY CALENDARED IN DEPARTMENTS 301/302 ARE HEREBY VACATED. WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)

James Chang, Assistant General Counsel, 415-538-2381 for Plaintiff.
Doron Weinberg, 415-552-2703 for Defendant Daniel Everett who is also present.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. Plaintiff's Counsel reports the current status of Plaintiff's compliance with the Court's 1/16/20 order. The Court asks Defendant's Counsel more questions re: additional documents on the Court's order.

1:48 p.m. The Court takes a break so Counsel may speak with his client.

1:51 p.m. The Court resumes. Defendant informs the Court his client may need more time to search for additional documents. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Status Conference (To Address Scheduling And Other Matters) Per Order Filed 2/7/20)

Continued to 3/11/20 at 1:30 pm for Further Status Conference. Mr. Everett is to fully comply with the Court's 1/16/20 order by the close of business no later than 3/6/20. Further, Plaintiff's Counsel also has a week to consult with his IT team and staff re: extraction of materials from plaintiff's laptop and coordinate with Mr. Weinberg. In addition, the Court vacates the hearing date of 3/16/20. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Notice Of Hearing On Order To Show Cause
Off Calendar Jan-16-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Christina M. Lauridsen, Esq., Senior Trial Counsel, State Bar of California, T:415.538.2271, for petitioner/moving party;
Daniel Everett, T:415.891.9172, respondent, appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: Hearing held on PETITIONER STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA's Order To Show Cause Re: Jurisdiction Over Law Practice Of Daniel Everett. The matter was argued.

After hearing, the Court ruled that Petitioner's request for an interim order assuming jurisdiction over the law practice of Daniel Everett is granted.

Mr. Everett's oral requests for a continuance and for the court to appoint an attorney are denied.

The Court sets a further hearing on March 16, 2020 at 9:30am on the Order To Show Cause and the Status of Implementation of the Court's order. Parties may file additional briefings no later than March 6, 2020.

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
